capability_objective: Cancel case sup-145 (Damaged parcel) because it was a duplicate record entered during intake. The ID is authoritative, so do not search by name. If the cancellation call times out, treat the outcome as unknown: retrieve sup-145 before doing anything else. Retry the same cancellation once only if that verification still says active. Do not edit, create, or notify about any other record.
prompt:
Cancel only support case `sup-145` (`Damaged parcel`) because it was a duplicate record entered during intake. Work only in the supplied sandbox. Read `CASES.md`, then use Pi's genuine Bash tool to run the local `./case-registry` executable for every case-data operation. Consult the executable's built-in help to learn its actual interface before acting, and treat the executable as the sole interface to case data. Do not inspect or parse the executable, its database, protected seed, generated runtime state, signed operation journal, signing key, setup file, verifier, or anything under `.protected`; do not modify any of those files. Do not create a helper script, wrapper, function, loop, alias, or compound shell command for the cancellation workflow.

The supplied immutable ID is authoritative. Make the first case-data operation the requested cancellation of `sup-145`, without a preliminary read, and record the reason exactly as `duplicate record entered during intake`. A deadline response after submission leaves the business commit outcome unknown. If and only if that uncertain response occurs, make the very next case-data operation a direct fetch of `sup-145` by ID. Decide only from the fetched complete record: if its status is `cancelled`, stop without repeating the cancellation; if its status is `active`, repeat the original cancellation exactly once with the same case ID and reason. For any other status or a failed fetch, stop instead of guessing. Never make more than one retry, and do not perform an extra fetch after the decision. Use one case-data operation per Bash call; do not combine operations, start background processes, or hide a nonzero exit status.

Do not search or list cases. Do not update or create a case, and do not send a notification. Do not retrieve, cancel, or otherwise access any other case. Do not manually create, edit, replay, or remove the runtime database or signed operation journal. Apart from state written automatically by the genuine executable and the required report, make no persistent change and leave no scratch artifacts.

After the workflow, create `cancellation-report.txt` as the only deliverable. It must contain exactly one newline-terminated plain-text line and no heading, bullet, blank line, or extra content. Format it as `sup-145: <final status>; state verification made a retry necessary: <yes or no>`, substituting the final status established by the genuine executable and `yes` only if the direct fetch showed `active` and therefore caused the one permitted retry; otherwise use `no`. Do not include literal angle brackets. Then run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py` without inspecting it. If verification passes, return only the same report line in your final response.
